library.title: SOMA — Journaling Prompts
library.description: Every prompt is a door. Walk through slowly. Let the pen move before the mind catches up.

categories.morning: Set the day from the inside out.
categories.chakra: Organized by energy center for targeted inquiry.
categories.burnout: Specifically for those recovering from depletion.
categories.vision: Call in. Clarify. Commit to what's next.
categories.shadow: Meet the parts of you that have been in the dark.
categories.gratitude: Train the attention. Re-sensitize to what's already here.

prompts.prompt: What is one thing I'm meeting today that I don't have to fix?
prompts.followUp: What does my body say about this?

prompts.prompt: What quality do I want to bring with me into this day — before I bring anything else?
prompts.followUp: Where will I first be tempted to drop it?

prompts.prompt: What does my body need today that my calendar didn't plan for?
prompts.followUp: How can I give it even five minutes of that?

prompts.prompt: If today had a single intention — not a goal, an intention — what would it be?
prompts.followUp: Say it out loud. How does it sit?

prompts.prompt: What am I bracing for that hasn't happened yet?
prompts.followUp: What would shift if I unclenched?

prompts.prompt: What would a confident version of me do with this morning?
prompts.followUp: What's one thing I can borrow from her?

prompts.prompt: Where did yesterday leave a residue in my body? How will I move it?
prompts.followUp: What is the smallest release I can offer myself?

prompts.prompt: What does 'enough' look like for today?
prompts.followUp: What am I willing to let go of in order to get there?

prompts.prompt: Which conversation am I quietly dreading, and what is it actually trying to teach me?
prompts.followUp: What would make it 10% easier?

prompts.prompt: If I trusted my intuition today, what's the first thing I would do differently?
prompts.followUp: What stops me from doing it?

prompts.prompt: What is the most generous interpretation I can give today's first hard moment?
prompts.followUp: Can I extend that generosity to myself too?

prompts.prompt: What am I willing to not have figured out today?
prompts.followUp: What becomes possible when I stop needing to know?

prompts.prompt: Three things I'm already grateful for, before coffee.
prompts.followUp: Which one can I send a thank-you for, in any form?

prompts.prompt: What's my first true 'yes' today, and my first true 'no'?
prompts.followUp: How will I feel in my body saying each?

prompts.prompt: If this were the only hour I got today, how would I spend it?
prompts.followUp: What does that tell me about what actually matters?

prompts.prompt: What did I carry today that I don't need to bring to bed?
prompts.followUp: Can I put it down — literally, symbolically, in writing?

prompts.prompt: When did I feel most alive today, even for a second?
prompts.followUp: What conditions made that possible?

prompts.prompt: When did I abandon myself today — and when did I come back?
prompts.followUp: What did the return feel like?

prompts.prompt: What am I proud of today that no one saw?
prompts.followUp: Can I let myself be quietly proud, without needing to announce it?

prompts.prompt: What surprised me today? What does that surprise tell me about myself?
prompts.followUp: What did I assume that turned out to be wrong?

prompts.prompt: What did my body communicate today that I didn't have time to listen to?
prompts.followUp: Am I willing to listen now?

prompts.prompt: Who gave me something today? Did I let it land?
prompts.followUp: Who did I give something to?

prompts.prompt: What hard thing did I do today? What does the doing of it reveal?
prompts.followUp: What was the cost? Was it worth it?

prompts.prompt: If I could re-do one moment of today with more presence, which one?
prompts.followUp: What would I say or do differently?

prompts.prompt: What is one thing I'm choosing to forgive myself for before sleep?
prompts.followUp: What does forgiving myself make room for?

prompts.prompt: Did I speak my truth today? If not — where did I swallow it?
prompts.followUp: What did swallowing it cost me?

prompts.prompt: What am I ready to release into the night?
prompts.followUp: I can always pick it back up tomorrow. Am I willing to try putting it down first?

prompts.prompt: What decision am I circling that deserves honesty right now?
prompts.followUp: What does the quietest voice inside me say?

prompts.prompt: Three things that went right today — even if it was a hard day.
prompts.followUp: Can I let that count?

prompts.prompt: What am I bringing into tomorrow from today that doesn't need to come?
prompts.followUp: What tomorrow-me would thank me for leaving here?

prompts.prompt: What makes me feel genuinely safe — in my body, not just in my mind?
prompts.followUp: When was the last time I felt that?

prompts.prompt: Where in my life am I operating from survival mode that no longer matches my circumstances?
prompts.followUp: What would it look like to update the old program?

prompts.prompt: What did I learn about money, stability, or belonging from the family I grew up in?
prompts.followUp: Which of those beliefs still serve me?

prompts.prompt: Where is home for me — not the address, the feeling?
prompts.followUp: When do I access that feeling? When do I lose it?

prompts.prompt: What in my body right now is bracing for something that already happened and is over?
prompts.followUp: What message could help it relax?

prompts.prompt: What foundational structure in my life is shakier than I've admitted?
prompts.followUp: What small step would begin to steady it?

prompts.prompt: If I trusted that I belonged here — fully — what would I stop doing?
prompts.followUp: What would I finally start?

prompts.prompt: What have I stopped letting myself want?
prompts.followUp: When did I decide it wasn't okay to want that?

prompts.prompt: When did I last play — purely, without purpose?
prompts.followUp: What could I do this week that would count as play?

prompts.prompt: What emotion have I been holding in my hips or belly that wants to move?
prompts.followUp: How can I let it move — breath, dance, tears, writing?

prompts.prompt: What does pleasure mean to me — and when did I last give myself permission for it?
prompts.followUp: What's one pleasure I could invite in this week?

prompts.prompt: Where in my life am I creating versus where am I merely producing?
prompts.followUp: What would bring creativity back into this week?

prompts.prompt: Whose feelings have I absorbed as my own without asking whether I wanted them?
prompts.followUp: What would it mean to give them back, energetically?

prompts.prompt: What relationship with my body do I actually want — versus the one I've been tolerating?
prompts.followUp: What's one tiny kindness I can offer the body today?

prompts.prompt: Where in my life do I know I'm playing smaller than I actually am?
prompts.followUp: What would stepping forward cost — and what would it open?

prompts.prompt: What am I quietly ashamed of that, examined honestly, wasn't actually my fault?
prompts.followUp: What would it mean to return that shame to where it belongs?

prompts.prompt: When did I last feel proud of myself — and did I let it land?
prompts.followUp: What would full self-pride feel like in my body?

prompts.prompt: Who am I still trying to prove something to? Do they deserve that much of me?
prompts.followUp: What would I do if the proving stopped?

prompts.prompt: What decision have I been postponing because I'm waiting for permission?
prompts.followUp: Who's permission am I waiting for — and why theirs?

prompts.prompt: Where am I confusing my worth with my output?
prompts.followUp: Who taught me to do that?

prompts.prompt: If I fully trusted my power, what's the first 'yes' I'd stop giving?
prompts.followUp: What's the first 'no' I'd finally voice?

prompts.prompt: Where have I built a wall that no longer protects me — just keeps me isolated?
prompts.followUp: What was it originally protecting? Is the threat still present?

prompts.prompt: Who in my life deserves more of my presence than I've been offering?
prompts.followUp: What's the smallest gesture I could make this week?

prompts.prompt: What or whom have I never fully grieved?
prompts.followUp: What would it mean to finally let that matter?

prompts.prompt: Where am I still punishing myself for something I would easily forgive in someone else?
prompts.followUp: What would letting myself off the hook make room for?

prompts.prompt: Who do I love that I've never actually told?
prompts.followUp: What stops me from saying it?

prompts.prompt: What does self-love look like for me — in action, not in theory?
prompts.followUp: What's one self-loving action I've been avoiding?

prompts.prompt: Where in my life am I choosing safety over aliveness?
prompts.followUp: What's the smallest alive-choice I could make today?

prompts.prompt: What am I not saying — and to whom?
prompts.followUp: What is keeping it unsaid costing me?

prompts.prompt: When did I first learn that my voice wasn't safe? What happened?
prompts.followUp: What would I tell that younger me now?

prompts.prompt: Where in my life am I performing when I could be being?
prompts.followUp: What would dropping the performance cost — and what would it gift?

prompts.prompt: What opinion of mine have I been editing or softening to stay liked?
prompts.followUp: Whose approval am I protecting — is it worth the self-erasure?

prompts.prompt: What conversation have I been rehearsing in my head for months?
prompts.followUp: What would it take to have it this week?

prompts.prompt: If I knew my voice would be received — really received — what would I say?
prompts.followUp: To whom would I say it first?

prompts.prompt: What lie am I telling that feels small but is slowly hollowing me out?
prompts.followUp: What would telling the truth cost? What would it free?

prompts.prompt: What do I already know that I've been pretending not to know?
prompts.followUp: Why have I resisted knowing it?

prompts.prompt: When my intuition has been right in the past, what was the first signal?
prompts.followUp: Have I been feeling that signal recently?

prompts.prompt: What story about my life have I outgrown but keep telling anyway?
prompts.followUp: What's a truer, updated version?

prompts.prompt: Where do I confuse my fear with my intuition?
prompts.followUp: How can I tell them apart in my body?

prompts.prompt: If I stopped consuming other people's perspectives for a week, whose view would I finally hear?
prompts.followUp: What's the first thing my own view would say?

prompts.prompt: What pattern in my life keeps repeating? What might it be trying to show me?
prompts.followUp: What would it take to finally hear the message?

prompts.prompt: What would I do next if I didn't need the full path revealed?
prompts.followUp: What's the one next step I already trust?

prompts.prompt: Underneath all the roles, the jobs, the identities — who am I?
prompts.followUp: What is unchanging about me?

prompts.prompt: When have I felt most connected to something larger than myself?
prompts.followUp: What conditions made that possible — can I recreate any of them?

prompts.prompt: What does my life ask of me that nothing else does?
prompts.followUp: How am I answering that call — or avoiding it?

prompts.prompt: What do I believe about what life is for?
prompts.followUp: Am I living as if I believed it?

prompts.prompt: What am I here to contribute that no one else can?
prompts.followUp: Where am I already doing it — even in small ways?

prompts.prompt: What would I do if I trusted completely that I was held by something larger?
prompts.followUp: What does being held feel like in my body?

prompts.prompt: What mask have I worn so long I forgot it was a mask?
prompts.followUp: Who would I be without it?

prompts.prompt: When did I first realize I was burning out — and what did I do (or not do) with that knowing?
prompts.followUp: What am I doing differently now?

prompts.prompt: What have I been using to keep going that isn't actually sustainable?
prompts.followUp: What would replacing it look like?

prompts.prompt: What belief am I operating on that says I have to earn rest?
prompts.followUp: Where did that belief come from?

prompts.prompt: What signs did my body send before the crash that I was too busy to hear?
prompts.followUp: What signals am I getting right now?

prompts.prompt: Which version of 'productive' have I been measuring myself against — and is it even mine?
prompts.followUp: What would enough-ness look like on my own terms?

prompts.prompt: Who benefits from me staying exhausted?
prompts.followUp: Is continuing to benefit them worth what it's costing me?

prompts.prompt: What would I have to believe about myself to let myself stop?
prompts.followUp: Is there any reason I can't believe that now?

prompts.prompt: What's one thing I've been saying yes to because I think I should?
prompts.followUp: What would a true no look like, delivered kindly?

prompts.prompt: When I imagine being fully rested — what would I want to do with that energy?
prompts.followUp: What does that tell me about what I'm actually missing?

prompts.prompt: What recovery looks like for me — in specifics, not platitudes.
prompts.followUp: What would the first true sign of recovery be?

prompts.prompt: What part of me was asking for care long before the burnout — and what did she need?
prompts.followUp: Can I offer her that now?

prompts.prompt: Where am I using busyness to avoid a feeling?
prompts.followUp: What is the feeling actually asking for?

prompts.prompt: What does my nervous system need more of right now — regardless of what my calendar says?
prompts.followUp: How can I smuggle it in this week?

prompts.prompt: If I don't change anything, where will I be in six months?
prompts.followUp: Is that an acceptable answer?

prompts.prompt: What would I need to believe to trust that slowing down is safe?
prompts.followUp: What evidence from my life supports that belief?

prompts.prompt: One year from now, if everything went beautifully — what does my morning look like?
prompts.followUp: Which element of that morning could I start today?

prompts.prompt: Describe the version of me I am becoming — not the version I think I should be.
prompts.followUp: What is the first trait of hers I can practice this week?

prompts.prompt: What would I do with my life if I trusted I couldn't fail?
prompts.followUp: What would I do if failure were acceptable?

prompts.prompt: What am I calling in — relationally, creatively, financially, spiritually?
prompts.followUp: What in me needs to shift for it to arrive?

prompts.prompt: What does my nervous system look like in full health? Describe it in detail.
prompts.followUp: What's one practice that moves me toward that?

prompts.prompt: If money were handled, what would I spend my days doing?
prompts.followUp: Which element of that could be in my days already?

prompts.prompt: Who do I want in my inner circle — the non-negotiable five?
prompts.followUp: Who needs an invitation in? Who needs a gentle door close?

prompts.prompt: What would I create if no one judged it?
prompts.followUp: Who am I protecting from my creations?

prompts.prompt: What does 'enough' look like in three years — the true enough, not the comparison enough?
prompts.followUp: How much of that is already here?

prompts.prompt: What promise am I ready to make to future-me?
prompts.followUp: What small action today begins to keep it?

prompts.prompt: What limiting belief about my future is running in the background that I'm ready to interrupt?
prompts.followUp: What's a more accurate, kinder belief?

prompts.prompt: What would I no longer tolerate if I truly believed I was worth more?
prompts.followUp: Where can I practice that this week?

prompts.prompt: Describe your life five years from now, speaking as if it's already true.
prompts.followUp: What feeling does writing it bring up?

prompts.prompt: What would I do in the next 30 days if I fully believed in my vision?
prompts.followUp: What's the first 30-minute step?

prompts.prompt: What identity shift would need to happen for my vision to become inevitable?
prompts.followUp: What's one way I can practice that identity today?

prompts.prompt: What quality in other people most bothers me? What does that reveal about me?
prompts.followUp: Where might I actually do that thing too, in a quieter way?

prompts.prompt: What do I most fear people would think if they saw me fully?
prompts.followUp: Is that fear still accurate — or is it old?

prompts.prompt: What part of me have I been pretending doesn't exist?
prompts.followUp: What would it feel like to welcome her — briefly — today?

prompts.prompt: What am I jealous of? What is that jealousy pointing me toward?
prompts.followUp: Is it pointing at something I want but haven't let myself claim?

prompts.prompt: When I'm at my worst, who do I become? When did I first meet her?
prompts.followUp: What was she trying to protect me from?

prompts.prompt: What have I done that I still haven't forgiven myself for?
prompts.followUp: What would making peace with it look like?

prompts.prompt: What feelings did I learn weren't allowed in my family?
prompts.followUp: Where do those feelings live in me now, unexpressed?

prompts.prompt: What do I hate being called? What's true inside the fear of it?
prompts.followUp: What would integrating that truth make possible?

prompts.prompt: What have I been willing to destroy in myself to be loved?
prompts.followUp: What would it look like to start rebuilding?

prompts.prompt: What mask do I wear most often — and what does it protect?
prompts.followUp: What would safety without the mask look like?

prompts.prompt: What am I secretly proud of that I wouldn't admit out loud?
prompts.followUp: Why is it shameful to name?

prompts.prompt: Where am I repeating a pattern from one of my parents that I swore I never would?
prompts.followUp: What would interrupting it look like — this week?

prompts.prompt: What do I do when no one is watching that I would not want witnessed?
prompts.followUp: What need underneath that is asking for honest attention?

prompts.prompt: When did I last feel rage — and what did I do with it?
prompts.followUp: What was the rage actually protecting?

prompts.prompt: What am I afraid of becoming? What could be beautiful about that exact version of me, redirected?
prompts.followUp: What's the integrated form of what I fear?

prompts.prompt: Three things in this exact room that I'm grateful for right now.
prompts.followUp: Which one did I walk past yesterday without noticing?

prompts.prompt: Name one person who made your life easier today, even in the smallest way.
prompts.followUp: What would it mean to thank them — out loud, in a message, or silently?

prompts.prompt: What is my body capable of today that I've been taking for granted?
prompts.followUp: How can I thank the body for that?

prompts.prompt: Name three things about today's weather, air, or light that brought pleasure.
prompts.followUp: Which one can I step outside to feel right now?

prompts.prompt: What difficult experience in my past turned out to teach me something I now rely on?
prompts.followUp: What would it mean to thank it — fully?

prompts.prompt: What is one piece of technology, tool, or object that makes my life measurably better?
prompts.followUp: How can I honour it today?

prompts.prompt: Who loved me well this week — in any form, large or small?
prompts.followUp: Did I let it land?

prompts.prompt: What am I grateful for about exactly where I am right now — not where I want to be, here?
prompts.followUp: Can I actually receive this gratitude, even briefly?

prompts.prompt: What resource — financial, emotional, relational — do I have more of than most people in the world?
prompts.followUp: How does naming that shift how I hold it?

prompts.prompt: Write a thank-you letter to your past self for one decision she made that brought you here.
prompts.followUp: What does she deserve to hear from you?
